what: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
text: stacbuildr R package creating STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) metadata. STAC open standard describing geospatial data way makes indexable, searchable, interoperable. package implements STAC specification version 1.1.0 using S7 classes outputs valid STAC JSON.

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("stevenpawley/stacbuildr")

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
text: STAC catalog hierarchy three object types: object contains links (JSON pointers connecting hierarchy) Items contain assets (references actual data files).

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
code: library(stacbuildr)  # Root catalog catalog <- stac_catalog(   id          = "my-catalog",   title       = "My Satellite Imagery Catalog",   description = "A catalog of satellite imagery for environmental monitoring" )  # Collection (extends catalog with extent, license, etc.) collection <- stac_collection(   id          = "sentinel-2-l2a",   title       = "Sentinel-2 Level-2A",   description = "Bottom-of-atmosphere reflectance imagery from Sentinel-2",   license     = "proprietary",   extent      = stac_extent(     spatial_bbox       = list(c(-180, -90, 180, 90)),     temporal_interval  = list(list("2015-06-27T00:00:00Z", NULL))  # NULL = ongoing   ),   keywords  = c("sentinel", "esa", "optical"),   providers = list(     stac_provider(name = "ESA", roles = c("producer", "licensor"),                   url  = "https://earth.esa.int")   ) )  # Item (a single scene / data granule) item <- stac_item(   id       = "S2A_MSIL2A_20230615",   geometry = list(type = "Polygon", coordinates = list(list(     c(-105.5, 39.5), c(-104.5, 39.5), c(-104.5, 40.5),     c(-105.5, 40.5), c(-105.5, 39.5)   ))),   bbox     = c(-105.5, 39.5, -104.5, 40.5),   datetime = "2023-06-15T10:30:00Z",   properties = list(platform = "sentinel-2a", instruments = c("msi"), gsd = 10) )

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
code: # Add standard navigation links catalog <- catalog |>   add_self_link("https://example.com/catalog.json") |>   add_root_link("https://example.com/catalog.json")  # Add a child collection to the catalog catalog <- add_child(catalog, collection)  # Add an item to a collection (with bidirectional links) collection <- add_item(   collection, item,   add_parent_links = TRUE,   parent_href = "./collection.json",   root_href   = "../catalog.json" )  # Add arbitrary links collection <- add_link(collection, rel = "license",                         href = "https://sentinel.esa.int/legal-notice.html",                         type = "text/html")

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
code: item <- item |>   add_eo_extension(     bands = list(       eo_band(name = "B4", common_name = "red",   center_wavelength = 0.665),       eo_band(name = "B3", common_name = "green", center_wavelength = 0.560),       eo_band(name = "B2", common_name = "blue",  center_wavelength = 0.490)     ),     cloud_cover = 5.2,     asset_key   = "visual"   # attach bands to a specific asset   )  # Pre-built band definitions for common sensors item <- add_eo_extension(item, bands = sentinel2_msi_bands()) item <- add_eo_extension(item, bands = landsat_oli_bands(include_thermal = TRUE))

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
code: # Create a STAC Item directly from a GeoTIFF (via terra) item <- item_from_stars(   file                 = "path/to/image.tif",   datetime             = "2023-06-15T10:30:00Z",   add_raster_bands     = TRUE,   calculate_statistics = FALSE )  # Create a STAC Item from an sf object library(sf) boundary <- st_read("boundary.shp") item <- item_from_sf(   boundary,   id       = "study-area",   datetime = "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z" )  # Batch-create items from a directory of rasters items <- items_from_directory("path/to/rasters", pattern = "\\.tif$")  # Calculate a collection extent from a list of items automatically extent <- extent_from_items(items)

title: An R package to Create Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalogs (STAC)
text: written directory structure follows STAC conventions:
code: # Write the entire catalog hierarchy to disk as JSON files write_stac(catalog, path = "output/stac")  # Write as an absolute-URL catalog (for web hosting) write_stac(catalog, path = "output/stac",            catalog_type = "absolute",            base_url     = "https://example.com/stac")  # Write individual objects write_catalog(collection, file = "collection.json") write_item(item, file = "items/my-item.json")  # Read back from disk catalog    <- read_stac("output/stac/catalog.json") collection <- read_stac("output/stac/collection/collection.json") output/stac/   catalog.json   sentinel-2-l2a/     collection.json     items/       S2A_MSIL2A_20230615.json

what: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: Adds Electro-Optical (EO) Extension STAC Item. EO data considered data represents snapshot Earth single date time. consist multiple spectral bands part electromagnetic spectrum. Important Note STAC 1.1.0 Changes: extension formerly field eo:bands, removed favor general field bands STAC common metadata. structure array Band Objects fields EO extension now eo: prefix, general fields like description moved common metadata need prefix.

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
code: add_eo_extension(   item,   bands = NULL,   cloud_cover = NULL,   snow_cover = NULL,   asset_key = NULL )

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: item STAC Item object created stac_item(). bands (list, optional) list band objects created eo_band(). band describes characteristics spectral band. asset multiple bands, provide list one entry per band order. cloud_cover (numeric, optional) Estimate cloud cover percentage (0-100). include valid data regions, excluding nodata areas. available calculated, provided. snow_cover (numeric, optional) Estimate snow/ice cover percentage (0-100) entire scene. include valid data regions. available, provided. asset_key (character, optional) provided, adds bands specific asset rather item properties. cloud_cover snow_cover properties always added item properties (assets) typically apply entire scene.

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: modified STAC Item EO extension fields added.

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: EO Extension v1.1.0 schema URI : https://stac-extensions.github.io/eo/v1.1.0/schema.json

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: band can contain following EO-specific fields (eo: prefix): eo:common_name: Common name band (e.g., "red", "green", "blue", "nir") eo:center_wavelength: Center wavelength micrometers eo:full_width_half_max: Full width half maximum (FWHM) micrometers eo:solar_illumination: Solar illumination band's wavelength Plus common metadata fields without prefix: name: Name band (e.g., "B01", "B02", "B1", "B5") description: Description band

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: EO extension defines standard common names typical spectral bands: Visible: "coastal", "blue", "green", "red" Red Edge: "rededge", "rededge071", "rededge075", "rededge078" Near Infrared: "nir", "nir08", "nir09" Short-wave Infrared: "cirrus", "swir16", "swir22" Long-wave Infrared: "lwir", "lwir11", "lwir12" Panchromatic: "pan"

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: important consider valid data regions, excluding "nodata" areas calculating coverages. Usually, cloud_cover snow_cover used Item Properties rather Item Assets, Item electro-optical source single snapshot Earth, coverages usually apply assets.

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: example, given band described (0.4um - 0.5um) eo:center_wavelength 0.45um eo:full_width_half_max 0.1um.

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: EO extension often used : Instrument Fields (common metadata): platform, instruments, constellation View Extension: view geometry (-nadir, azimuth, sun angles) Raster Extension: data type, nodata, scale/offset

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
text: EO Extension Specification: https://github.com/stac-extensions/eo

title: Add EO Extension to a STAC Item — add_eo_extension
code: # Create an item item <- stac_item(   id = "LC08_L2SP_001002_20230615",   geometry = list(     type = "Polygon",     coordinates = list(list(       c(-105.5, 39.5),       c(-104.5, 39.5),       c(-104.5, 40.5),       c(-105.5, 40.5),       c(-105.5, 39.5)     ))   ),   bbox = c(-105.5, 39.5, -104.5, 40.5),   datetime = "2023-06-15T10:30:00Z",   properties = list(     platform = "landsat-8",     instruments = c("oli", "tirs")   ) )  # Add EO extension with cloud cover item <- add_eo_extension(item, cloud_cover = 12.5)  # Create multispectral bands red_band <- eo_band(   name = "B4",   common_name = "red",   center_wavelength = 0.665,   full_width_half_max = 0.038 )  green_band <- eo_band(   name = "B3",   common_name = "green",   center_wavelength = 0.560,   full_width_half_max = 0.043 )  blue_band <- eo_band(   name = "B2",   common_name = "blue",   center_wavelength = 0.490,   full_width_half_max = 0.038 )  nir_band <- eo_band(   name = "B5",   common_name = "nir",   center_wavelength = 0.865,   full_width_half_max = 0.033 )  # Add bands to the item item <- item |>   add_asset(     "visual",     href = "https://example.com/LC08_visual.tif",     type = "image/tiff; application=geotiff",     roles = c("data")   ) |>   add_eo_extension(     bands = list(red_band, green_band, blue_band, nir_band),     cloud_cover = 5.2,     asset_key = "visual"   )  # Combine EO and Raster extensions combined_band <- eo_band(   name = "B4",   common_name = "red",   center_wavelength = 0.665,   full_width_half_max = 0.038,   description = "Red band (0.64-0.67)" )  # Add raster properties to the same band combined_band$nodata <- 0 combined_band$data_type <- "uint16" combined_band$`raster:spatial_resolution` <- 30 combined_band$`raster:scale` <- 0.0001 combined_band$`raster:offset` <- 0  item <- item |>   add_eo_extension(bands = list(combined_band)) |>   add_raster_extension(bands = list(combined_band))

what: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
text: Adds STAC Item Catalog Collection creating appropriate link relationship. function modifies catalog/collection object include "item" link pointing Item. Optionally, can also modify Item include links back parent ("parent", "collection", "root").

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
code: add_item(   catalog,   item,   href = NULL,   add_parent_links = FALSE,   parent_href = NULL,   root_href = NULL )

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
text: catalog STAC Catalog Collection object (created stac_catalog() stac_collection()). item STAC Item object (created stac_item()). Can also list Items add multiple items . href (character, optional) relative absolute path Item JSON file located. NULL (default), generates path based item's ID: "./items/{item@id}.json". Can vector paths item list. add_parent_links (logical, optional) TRUE, modifies Item(s) include reciprocal links back parent catalog. Adds "parent" "root" links. parent Collection, also adds "collection" link. Default FALSE avoid modifying original Item object. parent_href (character, optional) href parent catalog/collection. used add_parent_links = TRUE. provided "self" link exists catalog, uses ; otherwise uses placeholder. root_href (character, optional) href root catalog. used add_parent_links = TRUE. provided, attempts use catalog's "root" link defaults parent_href.

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
text: modified catalog/collection object Item link(s) added. add_parent_links = TRUE, also returns Item(s) updated links attribute named "items" (accessible via attr(result, "items")).

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
text: function creates "item" link catalog points Item. Based STAC specification, Items strongly recommended provide link STAC Collection, adding Items Collection, consider setting add_parent_links = TRUE. add_parent_links = TRUE: Adds "parent" link Item pointing catalog/collection Adds "root" link Item pointing root catalog parent Collection, adds "collection" link sets collection property Item (required collection link present)

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
text: can add multiple items passing list Item objects. case, href either: NULL auto-generate paths items vector length number items

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
text: Based STAC Item specification, Item must following fields: type: "Feature" stac_version: e.g., "1.1.0" id: Unique identifier within collection geometry: GeoJSON geometry null bbox: Bounding box (required geometry null) properties: Object metadata (must include datetime) links: Array links assets: Object describing available data files

title: Add an Item to a STAC Catalog or Collection — add_item
code: # Create a collection collection <- stac_collection(   id = "landsat-8",   description = "Landsat 8 imagery",   license = "CC0-1.0",   extent = stac_extent(     spatial_bbox = list(c(-180, -90, 180, 90)),     temporal_interval = list(list("2013-04-11T00:00:00Z", NULL))   ) )  # Create an item item <- stac_item(   id = "LC08_L1TP_001001_20200101_20200101_01_T1",   geometry = list(     type = "Polygon",     coordinates = list(list(       c(-180, -90), c(180, -90), c(180, 90), c(-180, 90), c(-180, -90)     ))   ),   bbox = c(-180, -90, 180, 90),   datetime = "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z",   properties = list() )  # Add the item to the collection (simple) collection <- add_item(collection, item)  # Add the item with parent links collection <- add_item(   collection,   item,   add_parent_links = TRUE,   parent_href = "./collection.json",   root_href = "../catalog.json" )  # Add multiple items items <- list(item, item, item) collection <- add_item(collection, items)  # Add multiple items with custom hrefs collection <- add_item(   collection,   items,   href = c("./2020/item1.json", "./2020/item2.json", "./2020/item3.json") )

what: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: Adds Raster Extension STAC Item modifies asset include raster-specific metadata. Raster Extension describes raster assets band level information data type, nodata values, scale/offset transforms, statistics. Important Note STAC 1.1.0 Changes: STAC 1.1.0, raster:bands field deprecated favor common bands construct merges functionality eo:bands raster:bands. raster-specific fields (like nodata, data_type, statistics, unit) now part STAC common metadata included directly band objects. remaining raster-specific fields (raster:sampling, raster:bits_per_sample, raster:spatial_resolution, raster:scale, raster:offset, raster:histogram) retain raster: prefix.

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
code: add_raster_extension(item, bands, asset_key = NULL)

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: item STAC Item object created stac_item(). bands list band objects created raster_band(). band describes characteristics single raster band (layer). asset multiple bands, provide list one entry per band order. asset_key (character, optional) provided, adds bands specific asset rather item properties. Useful different assets different band structures.

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: modified STAC Item raster extension fields added.

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: Raster Extension v1.1.0 schema URI : https://stac-extensions.github.io/raster/v1.1.0/schema.json

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: band can contain common metadata fields raster-specific fields: Common Metadata (prefix): nodata: Pixel values interpreted nodata data_type: Data type band (e.g., "uint8", "int16", "float32") unit: Unit measurement pixel values statistics: Object min, max, mean, stddev, valid_percent Raster-Specific (raster: prefix): raster:sampling: Pixel sampling method ("area" "point") raster:bits_per_sample: Actual number bits used per sample raster:spatial_resolution: Average spatial resolution meters raster:scale: Multiplicative scaling factor convert DN values raster:offset: Additive offset convert DN values raster:histogram: Histogram distribution pixel values

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: remote sensing, raster data often stores raw Digital Numbers (DN) must transformed physical values using: value = scale * DN + offset example, storing reflectance (0-1) integers (0-10000) scale=0.0001.

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: Supported data type values include: "int8", "int16", "int32", "int64" "uint8", "uint16", "uint32", "uint64" "float16", "float32", "float64" "cint16", "cint32" (complex integers) "cfloat32", "cfloat64" (complex floats) "" (custom types)

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
text: Raster Extension Specification: https://github.com/stac-extensions/raster

title: Add Raster Extension to a STAC Item or Asset — add_raster_extension
code: # Create an item item <- stac_item(   id = "my-raster",   geometry = list(type = "Point", coordinates = c(-105, 40)),   bbox = c(-105, 40, -105, 40),   datetime = "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z" )  # Add a single-band raster asset band <- raster_band(   nodata = 0,   data_type = "uint16",   spatial_resolution = 30,   scale = 0.0001,   offset = 0 )  item <- add_raster_extension(item, bands = list(band))  # Add multi-band raster with statistics red_band <- raster_band(   nodata = 0,   data_type = "uint16",   spatial_resolution = 10,   scale = 0.0001,   offset = -0.1,   statistics = raster_statistics(     minimum = 1,     maximum = 10000,     mean = 2500,     stddev = 1200,     valid_percent = 99.5   ) )  green_band <- raster_band(   nodata = 0,   data_type = "uint16",   spatial_resolution = 10,   scale = 0.0001,   offset = -0.1 )  item <- item |>   add_asset(     key = "visual",     href = "https://example.com/image.tif",     type = "image/tiff; application=geotiff",     roles = c("data")   ) |>   add_raster_extension(     bands = list(red_band, green_band),     asset_key = "visual"   )

title: Create an EO Band Object — eo_band
text: name (character, optional) Name band given data provider (e.g., "B01", "B02", "B1", "B5", "QA"). common_name (character, optional) Common name band. one standard names applicable: "coastal", "blue", "green", "red", "rededge", "rededge071", "rededge075", "rededge078", "nir", "nir08", "nir09", "cirrus", "swir16", "swir22", "lwir", "lwir11", "lwir12", "pan". description (character, optional) Description fully explain band. CommonMark 0.29 syntax may used rich text representation. center_wavelength (numeric, optional) Center wavelength band micrometers. example, red band might 0.665. full_width_half_max (numeric, optional) Full width half maximum (FWHM) band, micrometers. width band measured half maximum transmission. solar_illumination (numeric, optional) Solar illumination value band, measured top atmosphere. Used atmospheric correction radiometric calibration. ... Additional fields band object. Can include fields extensions like raster fields (nodata, data_type, raster:scale, etc.).

title: Create an EO Band Object — eo_band
text: list representing EO band object.

title: Create an EO Band Object — eo_band

title: Create an EO Band Object — eo_band
text: Wavelengths specified micrometers. example: Blue: ~0.49 (490 nm) Green: ~0.56 (560 nm) Red: ~0.66 (660 nm) NIR: ~0.86 (860 nm)

title: Create an EO Band Object — eo_band

title: Create an EO Band Object — eo_band
code: # Simple band with common name band <- eo_band(   name = "B4",   common_name = "red" )  # Band with full wavelength specification band <- eo_band(   name = "B8",   common_name = "nir",   center_wavelength = 0.865,   full_width_half_max = 0.033,   description = "Near Infrared band (0.85-0.88)" )  # Band with solar illumination band <- eo_band(   name = "B2",   common_name = "blue",   center_wavelength = 0.490,   full_width_half_max = 0.065,   solar_illumination = 1959.66 )  # Combine with raster metadata band <- eo_band(   name = "B4",   common_name = "red",   center_wavelength = 0.665,   full_width_half_max = 0.038,   # Raster fields (no prefix for common fields)   nodata = 0,   data_type = "uint16",   # Raster-specific fields (with prefix)   "raster:spatial_resolution" = 30,   "raster:scale" = 0.0001,   "raster:offset" = 0 )

title: Create a Raster Band Object — raster_band
text: nodata (numeric NULL, optional) Pixel value(s) interpreted "data". Can single value vector values. Common values: 0, -9999, NaN. data_type (character, optional) Data type band. Must one : "int8", "int16", "int32", "int64", "uint8", "uint16", "uint32", "uint64", "float16", "float32", "float64", "cint16", "cint32", "cfloat32", "cfloat64", "". unit (character, optional) Unit measurement pixel values. Examples: "m" (meters), "W sr-1 m-2" (radiance), "1" (unitless/reflectance). statistics (list, optional) Statistics object created raster_statistics() describing distribution pixel values. sampling (character, optional) Pixel sampling method. Either "area" (pixel represents area) "point" (pixel represents point). Default "area". bits_per_sample (integer, optional) Actual number bits used band. needed different standard data type (e.g., 1-bit data stored uint8). spatial_resolution (numeric, optional) Average spatial resolution pixels band, meters. Useful resolution varies differs ground sample distance (gsd). scale (numeric, optional) Multiplicative scaling factor transform pixel values: physical_value = scale * DN + offset. Default 1. offset (numeric, optional) Additive offset transform pixel values: physical_value = scale * DN + offset. Default 0. histogram (list, optional) Histogram object created raster_histogram() describing distribution pixel values. ... Additional fields band object. Can include fields extensions like "eo:common_name", "eo:center_wavelength".

title: Create a Raster Band Object — raster_band
text: list representing raster band object.

title: Create a Raster Band Object — raster_band
code: # Simple band with just data type band <- raster_band(data_type = "uint8")  # Band with nodata and scale/offset band <- raster_band(   nodata = 0,   data_type = "uint16",   scale = 0.0001,   offset = 0,   unit = "1" )  # Band with statistics band <- raster_band(   nodata = -9999,   data_type = "int16",   spatial_resolution = 30,   statistics = raster_statistics(     minimum = -1000,     maximum = 8000,     mean = 2500,     stddev = 1500   ) )  # Band combining raster and EO extension band <- raster_band(   nodata = 0,   data_type = "uint16",   scale = 0.0001,   offset = -0.1,   spatial_resolution = 10,   unit = "1",   "eo:common_name" = "red",   "eo:center_wavelength" = 0.665,   "eo:full_width_half_max" = 0.038 )

text: href (character, required) URI asset object. Can relative absolute. Examples: "./data/image.tif", "https://example.com/image.tif". title (character, optional) Displayed title asset. description (character, optional) Description asset. type (character, optional) Media type asset. Examples: "image/tiff; application=geotiff", "image/png", "application/json". Additional fields asset. allows extension-specific properties like "eo:bands", "raster:bands", "proj:shape", etc.

title: Create a STAC Catalog — stac_catalog
text: id (character, required) Identifier Catalog. Must unique within parent catalog one exists. contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores. field required STAC specification. description (character, required) Detailed multi-line description fully explain Catalog. field provide comprehensive information catalog's contents, purpose, scope. field required STAC specification. title (character, optional) short descriptive one-line title Catalog. Recommended human-readable identification. stac_version (character, required) STAC version Catalog implements. Defaults "1.1.0". field required STAC specification. type (character, optional) Must set "Catalog" catalogs. Defaults "Catalog". collections, "Collection". field required STAC specification. stac_extensions (character vector, optional) list extension URLs Catalog implements. Extensions listed must contain extensions extend Catalog specification , extensions Items Collections. extension full URI extension's JSON schema. Default NULL (extensions). conformsTo (character vector, optional) list URIs declaring conformance STAC API specifications standards. Typically used catalog served via API. Introduced STAC 1.1.0. Default NULL. ... Additional fields include catalog. extra named arguments added catalog object. allows custom extensions additional metadata beyond core specification.

title: Create a STAC Catalog — stac_catalog
text: Catalogs use links connect STAC resources. Common link relation types include: root: URL root STAC Catalog Collection self: Absolute URL current catalog file parent: URL parent STAC Catalog Collection child: URL child STAC Catalog Collection item: URL STAC Item Use helper functions add_self_link(), add_root_link(), add_parent_link(), add_child(), add_item() manage links creating catalog. self link root link strongly recommended. Non-root Catalogs include parent link.

title: Create a STAC Catalog — stac_catalog
text: STAC Catalog Specification: https://github.com/radiantearth/stac-spec/blob/master/catalog-spec/catalog-spec.md

title: Create a STAC Catalog — stac_catalog
code: # Create a basic catalog catalog <- stac_catalog(   id = "my-catalog",   description = "A catalog of satellite imagery for environmental monitoring" )  # Create a catalog with all optional fields catalog <- stac_catalog(   id = "north-america-imagery",   title = "North America Satellite Imagery",   description = paste(     "A comprehensive catalog of satellite imagery covering North America",     "from various sensors including Landsat, Sentinel, and commercial",     "providers. Data spans from 2013 to present."   ),   stac_version = "1.1.0" )  # Add links to the catalog catalog <- catalog |>   add_self_link("https://example.com/catalog.json") |>   add_root_link("https://example.com/catalog.json")  # Add child catalogs landsat_catalog <- stac_catalog(   id = "landsat",   description = "Landsat satellite imagery" )  catalog <- add_child(   catalog,   landsat_catalog,   href = "./landsat/catalog.json",   title = "Landsat Imagery" )  # Create a catalog with a custom extension catalog_with_version <- stac_catalog(   id = "versioned-catalog",   description = "A catalog with version tracking",   stac_extensions = c(     "https://stac-extensions.github.io/version/v1.2.0/schema.json"   ),   # Custom fields from the version extension   version = "1.0.0",   deprecated = FALSE )  # Convert to JSON catalog_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(as.list(catalog), auto_unbox = TRUE, pretty = TRUE) cat(catalog_json) #> { #>   "type": "Catalog", #>   "stac_version": "1.1.0", #>   "id": "north-america-imagery", #>   "description": "A comprehensive catalog of satellite imagery covering North America from various sensors including Landsat, Sentinel, and commercial providers. Data spans from 2013 to present.", #>   "title": "North America Satellite Imagery", #>   "links": [ #>     { #>       "rel": "self", #>       "href": "https://example.com/catalog.json", #>       "type": "application/json" #>     }, #>     { #>       "rel": "root", #>       "href": "https://example.com/catalog.json", #>       "type": "application/json" #>     }, #>     { #>       "rel": "child", #>       "href": "./landsat/catalog.json", #>       "type": "application/json", #>       "title": "Landsat Imagery" #>     } #>   ] #> }

title: Create a STAC Collection — stac_collection
text: Creates STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) Collection object following STAC specification version 1.1.0. Collection extends Catalog specification additional metadata helps enable discovery, including spatial temporal extents, license information, summaries data.

title: Create a STAC Collection — stac_collection
code: stac_collection(   id,   description,   license,   extent,   title = NULL,   stac_version = "1.1.0",   type = "Collection",   stac_extensions = NULL,   keywords = NULL,   providers = NULL,   links = list(),   summaries = NULL,   assets = NULL,   conformsTo = NULL,   ... )

title: Create a STAC Collection — stac_collection
text: id (character, required) Identifier Collection. Must unique across collections root catalog. contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores. description (character, required) Detailed multi-line description fully explain Collection. CommonMark 0.29 syntax may used rich text representation. provide comprehensive information collection's contents, purpose, scope. license (character, required) Collection's license(s) SPDX License identifier, "various", "". collection includes data multiple different licenses, use "various" add link license. STAC 1.1.0, "proprietary" deprecated favor "". Examples: "CC--4.0", "MIT", "". extent (list, required) Spatial temporal extents describe bounds Items contained within Collection. Must named list two elements: spatial: list element bbox - list one bounding boxes. bbox numeric vector 4 6 numbers: c(west, south, east, north) 2D c(west, south, min_elev, east, north, max_elev) 3D. first bbox describes overall spatial extent. temporal: list element interval - list one time intervals. interval character vector length 2 ISO 8601 datetime strings: list("start", "end"). Use NULL open-ended intervals (e.g., list("2020-01-01T00:00:00Z", NULL) ongoing data). Note: use list() c() — c() drops NULL, produce invalid interval. Use helper function stac_extent() create structure easily. title (character, optional) short descriptive one-line title Collection. Recommended human-readable identification. stac_version (character, optional) STAC version Collection implements. Defaults "1.1.0". type (character, optional) Must set "Collection". Defaults "Collection". stac_extensions (character vector, optional) list extension identifiers (URIs) Collection implements. Common extensions include Item Assets, Version, Scientific Citation, . full URI extension's JSON schema. Default NULL. keywords (character vector, optional) List keywords describing Collection. Helps discovery categorization. providers (list, optional) list Provider objects. provider list fields: name (required), description, roles (e.g., "producer", "licensor", "processor", "host"), url. Use helper function stac_provider() create providers. links (list, optional) array Link objects. Common link relations Collections include "self", "root", "parent", "item", "child", "license". Note Catalogs require least one item child link, required Collections (recommended). Defaults empty list. summaries (list, optional) map property summaries describe range values properties found Items Collection. Strongly recommended. property can summarized array unique values, range (minimum maximum), JSON Schema. Common properties summarize include "datetime", "platform", "instruments", "gsd", "eo:bands", etc. Use stac_summaries() helper create . assets (list, optional) Dictionary asset objects can downloaded Collection level (Item-specific assets). assets apply entire collection, preview images documentation. describing assets available Items, use Item Assets extension. conformsTo (character vector, optional) list URIs declaring conformance STAC API specifications standards. Introduced STAC 1.1.0. ... Additional fields include collection. allows custom extensions additional metadata beyond core specification.

title: Create a STAC Item — stac_item
text: Creates STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) Item object following STAC specification version 1.1.0. STAC Item GeoJSON Feature additional fields represents atomic collection inseparable data metadata. Items core building blocks STAC catalogs.

title: Create a STAC Item — stac_item
code: stac_item(   id,   geometry,   bbox = NULL,   datetime = NULL,   properties = list(),   assets = list(),   links = list(),   stac_version = "1.1.0",   type = "Feature",   stac_extensions = NULL,   collection = NULL,   start_datetime = NULL,   end_datetime = NULL,   ... )

title: Create a STAC Item — stac_item
text: id (character, required) Provider identifier Item. ID unique within Collection contains Item. recommended use data provider's existing identification scheme. geometry (list, required) Defines full footprint asset represented Item, formatted according RFC 7946, section 3.1 (geometry) section 3.2 (geometry). Must valid GeoJSON geometry object (e.g., Point, Polygon, MultiPolygon) NULL non-spatial items. Coordinates WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) (longitude, latitude) (longitude, latitude, elevation). bbox (numeric vector, required geometry NULL) Bounding Box asset represented Item, formatted according RFC 7946, section 5. Must numeric vector either 4 values c(west, south, east, north) 2D 6 values c(west, south, min_elev, east, north, max_elev) 3D. Required geometry NULL, prohibited geometry NULL. datetime (character, required unless start_datetime end_datetime provided) searchable date time assets, must UTC, formatted according RFC 3339, section 5.6. Use ISO 8601 format: "2020-01-01T12:00:00Z". Can NULL start_datetime end_datetime provided properties. properties (named list, optional) Additional metadata Item. properties named elements. Common properties include title, description, created, updated, platform, instruments, gsd, etc. datetime property automatically added datetime parameter. Default empty list. assets (named list, optional) Dictionary asset objects can downloaded accessed. asset created stac_asset(). Keys asset identifiers (e.g., "visual", "thumbnail"). Default empty list. links (list, optional) List link objects resources related URLs. Items strongly recommended provide link STAC Collection. Use add_link() related helper functions add links creation. Default empty list. stac_version (character, optional) STAC version Item implements. Defaults "1.1.0". type (character, optional) Type GeoJSON Object. MUST set "Feature". Defaults "Feature". stac_extensions (character vector, optional) list extension identifiers (URIs) Item implements. Common extensions include EO (Electro-Optical), SAR, projection, view extensions. full URI extension's JSON schema. Default NULL. collection (character, optional) ID STAC Collection Item references collection relation type links array. field required collection link present. Usually set automatically add_item() adding Collection. Default NULL. start_datetime (character, optional) Start datetime Items represent time range. Must UTC RFC 3339 format. used datetime NULL. end_datetime (character, optional) End datetime Items represent time range. Must UTC RFC 3339 format. used datetime NULL. ... Additional fields include properties object. merged properties parameter.

title: Create a STAC Item — stac_item
text: required field properties datetime, recommended add fields. Common metadata fields include: title: Short description description: Detailed description created: Creation time updated: Last update time platform: Satellite/platform name instruments: Sensor instruments used gsd: Ground Sample Distance (resolution meters) constellation: Satellite constellation

title: Create a STAC Item — stac_item
code: # Basic Item with point geometry item <- stac_item(   id = "observation-001",   geometry = list(     type = "Point",     coordinates = c(-105.0, 40.0)   ),   bbox = c(-105.0, 40.0, -105.0, 40.0),   datetime = "2023-06-15T10:30:00Z" )  # Item with polygon geometry and additional properties item <- stac_item(   id = "LC08_L1TP_044034_20230615",   geometry = list(     type = "Polygon",     coordinates = list(list(       c(-105.5, 39.5),       c(-104.5, 39.5),       c(-104.5, 40.5),       c(-105.5, 40.5),       c(-105.5, 39.5)     ))   ),   bbox = c(-105.5, 39.5, -104.5, 40.5),   datetime = "2023-06-15T17:30:00Z",   properties = list(     title = "Landsat 8 Scene",     platform = "landsat-8",     instruments = c("oli", "tirs"),     gsd = 30,     "eo:cloud_cover" = 5.2   ) )  # Add assets to the item item <- item |>   add_asset(     key = "visual",     href = "https://example.com/LC08_visual.tif",     title = "True Color Image",     type = "image/tiff; application=geotiff",     roles = c("visual")   ) |>   add_asset(     key = "thumbnail",     href = "https://example.com/LC08_thumb.png",     title = "Thumbnail",     type = "image/png",     roles = c("thumbnail")   )  # Item with time range (datetime is NULL) item <- stac_item(   id = "composite-2023-q2",   geometry = list(     type = "Polygon",     coordinates = list(list(       c(-180, -90), c(180, -90), c(180, 90), c(-180, 90), c(-180, -90)     ))   ),   bbox = c(-180, -90, 180, 90),   datetime = NULL,   start_datetime = "2023-04-01T00:00:00Z",   end_datetime = "2023-06-30T23:59:59Z",   properties = list(     title = "Q2 2023 Global Composite"   ) )  # Item with 3D bbox (including elevation) item <- stac_item(   id = "lidar-001",   geometry = list(     type = "Point",     coordinates = c(-105.0, 40.0, 1500)   ),   bbox = c(-105.0, 40.0, 1500, -105.0, 40.0, 1500),   datetime = "2023-06-15T14:00:00Z" )  # Non-spatial item (geometry and bbox are NULL) item <- stac_item(   id = "global-report-2023",   geometry = NULL,   bbox = NULL,   datetime = "2023-12-31T23:59:59Z",   properties = list(     title = "Annual Global Climate Report"   ) )  # Convert to JSON item_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(as.list(item), auto_unbox = TRUE, pretty = TRUE) cat(item_json) #> { #>   "type": "Feature", #>   "stac_version": "1.1.0", #>   "id": "global-report-2023", #>   "geometry": {}, #>   "properties": { #>     "title": "Annual Global Climate Report", #>     "datetime": "2023-12-31T23:59:59Z" #>   }, #>   "links": [], #>   "assets": [] #> }

Default NULL. method (character, optional) HTTP method use following link (e.g., "GET", "POST"). Default NULL. headers (list named vector, optional) HTTP headers include following link. Default NULL. body (list, optional) HTTP body include following link (typically used POST requests). Default NULL. merge (logical, optional) Whether merge link body current resource following link. Default FALSE.

what: Write a STAC Catalog Structure to Disk — write_stac

title: Write a STAC Catalog Structure to Disk — write_stac
text: catalog STAC Catalog Collection object created stac_catalog() stac_collection(). path (character, required) Root directory path catalog written. created exist. catalog_type (character, optional) Type catalog create. One : "self-contained": links use relative paths within catalog structure. Best portability publishing. "relative": Links use relative paths may reference external resources. "absolute": links use absolute URLs. Best web-served catalogs. Default "self-contained". overwrite (logical, optional) TRUE, overwrites existing files. FALSE, throws error files already exist. Default FALSE. pretty (logical, optional) TRUE, writes formatted JSON indentation. FALSE, writes compact JSON. Default TRUE. base_url (character, optional) Base URL absolute links catalog_type = "absolute". example, "https://example.com/stac". Required using absolute catalog type.

text: Invisibly returns path catalog written.

title: Write a STAC Catalog Structure to Disk — write_stac
text: Self-Contained Catalogs: links use relative paths referenced resources within catalog directory structure. portable option recommended sharing archiving catalogs. Relative Catalogs: Links use relative paths may reference resources outside catalog tree. Useful integrating existing file structures. Absolute Catalogs: links use absolute URLs. Required catalog served web server. Requires base_url specified.

title: Write a STAC Catalog Structure to Disk — write_stac
text: function creates directory structure based catalog hierarchy:
code: path/   catalog.json                    # Root catalog   collection1/     collection.json               # Collection     item1/       item1.json                  # Items (each in own subdirectory)     item2/       item2.json   collection2/     collection.json     subcatalog/       catalog.json

title: Write a STAC Catalog Structure to Disk — write_stac
text: use add_child() add_item(), child catalogs items automatically stored attributes parent catalog. write_stac() function retrieves stored objects writes recursively.

title: Write a STAC Catalog Structure to Disk — write_stac
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a catalog structure catalog <- stac_catalog(   id = "my-catalog",   description = "Example STAC catalog" )  collection <- stac_collection(   id = "landsat-8",   description = "Landsat 8 imagery",   license = "CC0-1.0",   extent = stac_extent(     spatial_bbox = list(c(-180, -90, 180, 90)),     temporal_interval = list(list("2013-04-11T00:00:00Z", NULL))   ) )  item <- stac_item(   id = "LC08_001",   geometry = my_geometry,   bbox = my_bbox,   datetime = "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z" )  # Add item to collection (automatically stored) collection <- add_item(collection, item)  # Add collection to catalog (automatically stored) catalog <- add_child(catalog, collection)  # Write entire structure - children and items are automatically written! write_stac(catalog, "output/stac")  # Write as absolute catalog for web serving write_stac(   catalog,   "output/stac",   catalog_type = "absolute",   base_url = "https://example.com/stac" )  # Overwrite existing catalog write_stac(catalog, "output/stac", overwrite = TRUE) } # }
